key_points:
### Key Compliance Points — Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy (Second Amendment) Directions, 2026
*(RBI/2025-26/241, dated 2026-03-10; effective from date of issue)*

- **What it does:** Amends **paragraph 77 – "Treatment of total counterparty credit risk (CCR)"** of the Capital Adequacy Directions, 2025, to add clarity and align with international standards (RBI/2025-26/241, 2026-03-10).
- **Para 77(1) (note added):** for consolidated capital computation, an AIFI must include CCR exposures of **all entities consolidated** under the "Scope of application" section (Chapter II).
- **Table 13 (substituted) — add-on factors for market-related off-balance-sheet items** (% by residual maturity), e.g. Interest Rate: **0.25 / 0.50 / 1.50**; Exchange Rate & Gold: **1.00 / 5.00 / 7.50**; Equities: **6.00 / 8.00 / 10.00**; Precious Metals ex-Gold: **7.00 / 7.00 / 8.00**; Other Commodities: **10.00 / 12.00 / 15.00** (for ≤1yr / >1–5yr / >5yr).
- **Note (b) (substituted):** for reset contracts, residual maturity = time to next reset; interest-rate contracts >1yr meeting the criteria carry an **add-on floor of 0.50%**.
- **New notes (e)–(g):** add-ons apply to **all** outstanding CCR exposures; clearing members of SEBI-recognised equity/commodity derivative segments must maintain CCR capital; definitions of "Precious Metals" (Silver, Platinum, Palladium) and "Other Commodities".
- **Para 77(6)(i)(a) (substituted):** **2% risk weight** on an AIFI's trade exposure to a **QCCP** (own or client clearing, where it must reimburse client losses on QCCP default); no capital needed if not obligated to reimburse, subject to a supporting legal opinion.
- **Commencement:** **from date of issue** (2026-03-10).

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
what_changed:
### What Changed
*(Amendment: RBI/2025-26/241, dated 2026-03-10 · effective from date of issue)*

| Provision | Change type | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| **Para 77(1)** | **Note inserted** | Consolidated CCR capital must include all consolidated entities' CCR exposures. |
| **Table 13 (para 77(2))** | **Substituted** | Revised add-on factors for market-related off-balance-sheet items by asset class and residual maturity. |
| **Note (b), para 77(2)** | **Substituted** | Reset-contract residual maturity rule + 0.50% add-on floor for qualifying >1yr interest-rate contracts. |
| **Notes (e)–(g), para 77(2)** | **Inserted** | Add-ons apply to all CCR exposures; clearing-member capital duty; asset-class definitions. |
| **Para 77(6)(i)(a)** | **Substituted** | 2% risk weight on trade exposure to a QCCP (with reimbursement condition + legal-opinion carve-out). |

- **Effective date:** from date of issue (2026-03-10).

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
implications:
**Why it matters for NaBFID:** These are technical capital rules for derivative and counterparty-credit exposures. If NaBFID uses interest-rate or currency derivatives (e.g. to hedge long-tenor infrastructure funding), the revised add-on factors and QCCP treatment change the capital it must hold, and consolidation now explicitly sweeps in subsidiaries' CCR.

*Illustrative example:* A >5-year interest-rate swap now carries a 1.50% add-on factor; exposure cleared through a Qualifying CCP attracts a 2% risk weight rather than a bilateral counterparty weight.

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
